ops.insert: Snack Line Shuffle — Question Audit Results
Audited: 2026-04-09 · Game: Snack Line Shuffle P1 · Rounds: R0–R13 (14 total) · Standard: CCSS.1.OA.C.6 · Grade: K–1 · Mechanic: drag-to-order, addition ≤5, largest-first
Critical bug fixed: R10 amber VO line violated Visual Neutrality Constraint. Amber trigger logic upgraded from 'any amber present' to 'transition-only'. All 14 round data verified correct.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['a','b']: 2+1=3>1+1=2 ✓. First addend order matches total order — no M1 trap by design. AmberLine: 'Hmm, are we in the right order?' — direction-free, no totals, no >/< ✓. All totals distinct (3,2) ✓. Within-5 ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — no action needed.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['a','b']: 3+1=4>1+2=3 ✓. First addend (3 vs 1) agrees with total order — no trap. AmberLine: 'I'm not sure I'm in the right spot.' — compliant ✓. Slightly larger numbers than R0 — good ramp ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — no action needed.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['c','b','a']: 3+2=5, 2+1=3, 1+1=2. First addends (1,2,3) agree with total order. Clean first 3-kid round. Widely spaced (2,3,5). AmberLine: 'Do you think I belong here?' ✓. No ties ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — no action needed.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['c','b','a']: 4+1=5, 2+2=4, 3+0=3. M1 trap ✓: 3+0 has bigger first addend (3) than 2+2 (2) but 3+0=3 < 2+2=4. Player sorting by first addend puts 3+0 ahead of 2+2 — wrong. AmberLine: 'Um… I think someone might be out of place.' — compliant ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — M1 trap mathematically valid.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['b','a','c']: 3+2=5, 4+0=4, 2+1=3. M1 trap ✓: 4+0 has biggest first addend (4) yet total (4) < 3+2=5. Strong top-position M1 trap. AmberLine: 'Are you sure about this spot?' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — M1 trap mathematically valid.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['c','b','a']: 1+3=4, 2+0=2, 1+0=1. Clean. Teaches second-addend-dominant expressions (1+3, first addend 1 but total 4). Widely spaced (1,2,4). AmberLine: 'Hmm, I'm not sure this is right.' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — pedagogically valuable clean round.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['b','a','c']: 3+2=5, 4+0=4, 1+1=2. M1 trap ✓: 4+0 has biggest first addend (4) but total 4 < 3+2=5. Same trap structure as R4 with different third kid (1+1=2 vs 2+1=3). Totals (2,4,5) well-spaced. AmberLine: 'Wait — should I be here?' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — valid M1 trap, distinct from R4.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['b','c','a']: 1+3=4, 0+3=3, 0+2=2. Clean. Two kids with zero first addend (0+3, 0+2) — first addend heuristic fails completely for them. Forces computation of both second addends. Totals (2,3,4) consecutive. AmberLine: 'Something feels off about this order.' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — pedagogically important: exposes limit of first-addend heuristic when first addends are 0.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['b','a','c']: 2+3=5, 3+0=3, 1+0=1. M1 trap ✓: 3+0 has bigger first addend (3) than 2+3 (2) but 3+0=3 < 2+3=5. Widely spaced totals (1,3,5). AmberLine: 'I don't think this neighbour and I are in the right order.' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — valid M1 trap, widely spaced totals make correct answer unambiguous once computed.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['d','c','b','a']: 2+3=5, 1+3=4, 2+1=3, 1+1=2. First 4-kid round. Clean — all totals consecutive (2,3,4,5). No M1 trap by design: establishes 4-kid ordering before adding traps. AmberLine: 'Hmm, are we lined up the right way?' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — correct clean intro to 4-kid block.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['c','a','b','d']: 2+3=5, 4+0=4, 3+0=3, 1+0=1. M1 double trap ✓: both 4+0 and 3+0 have bigger first addends than 2+3 but smaller totals. Strongest trap in set. DATA CORRECT. AMBER LINE VIOLATION FIXED: Old line 'I think someone nearby has more snacks than me' implied a directional comparison (who has more) — banned by Visual Neutrality Constraint and permanent amber feedback rule ('no >/< relation, no explicit who-goes-first'). Fixed to: 'Hmm, I'm not sure we've all been counted up properly.' — direction-free, prompts recomputation ✓.
attributes.@qa09: FAIL → FIXED: Amber line replaced. Also upgraded amber trigger to transition-only logic (spec requirement). No other action needed.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['a','b','c','d']: displayOrder=['b','a','c','d'] — starts slightly shuffled. 3+2=5, 4+0=4, 2+0=2, 1+0=1. M1 trap ✓: 4+0 bigger first addend (4) than 3+2 (3), but 4+0=4 < 3+2=5. Three trailing X+0 kids trivial to compute; challenge at top pair only. AmberLine: 'Wait — check our snack counts again!' — prompts recomputation, no direction ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — valid M1 trap. Good pattern: three +0 kids make the trap obviously at the leader pair.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['c','d','b','a']: 0+4=4, 0+3=3, 0+2=2, 0+1=1. All four kids have zero first addend — first addend heuristic completely useless. Player must read and compute all second addends. displayOrder ['a','b','c','d'] = [0+1, 0+2, 0+4, 0+3] — scrambled (not ascending). AmberLine: 'Are we in the right spots?' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — powerful pedagogical round: demonstrates that the second addend alone determines the total when first=0.

attributes.@qa08: correctOrder=['a','b','c','d']: displayOrder=['b','a','c','d'] — top pair swapped as starting position. 3+2=5, 4+0=4, 2+1=3, 1+0=1. M1 trap ✓: 4+0 bigger first addend (4) than 3+2 (3), but 4+0=4 < 3+2=5. Session-closing round. Totals (1,3,4,5) — gap between 1st and 2nd position larger (1 vs 3). AmberLine: 'Something's off — I'm not sure this is right.' ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — valid final-round M1 trap. Good close: top-pair reversal in displayOrder ensures M1 trap is the first thing the player must resolve.

ops.insert: SLS-M1 · Serve button — cosmetic only, never reveals total

attributes.@qa08: Spec: Serve tap is cosmetic confirmation only. Cannot be activated on partial/incorrect line. No total or ordering information revealed by Serve. Code: ServeButton disabled unless allGreen===true (line fully correct). handleServe only calls setPhase('served') on correct — no feedback on wrong. Celebration state shows expressions (not totals) ✓. PASS.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — cannot be used as a probe. Spec compliance verified.

ops.insert: SLS-M2 · Visual neutrality — totals never displayed in gameplay

attributes.@qa08: DraggableKidCard renders only kid.expression — never kid.total. Celebration state shows expressions + checkmark, no totals. Idle, HUD, connector, legend, amber toast — all total-free. Level browser has a toggle 'Reveal totals (internal)' for design reference — gated by showTotals state, does not appear in game. PASS — permanent design rule upheld.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — full string audit confirms zero total display in any gameplay state.

attributes.@qa08: Spec: 'Do not play VO if the pair was already amber — transition only.' Old code: `if (hasAmber && amberMsg === null)` — fires whenever amber is present and no toast is showing (not true transition logic: a dismissed toast could re-fire on the same amber pair). Fixed: prevAmberPairsRef tracks which pair-keys were amber on the previous drag. Toast only fires if currentAmberPairs contains at least one key not in prevAmberPairsRef — true transition-only behavior. prevAmberPairsRef reset in startRound for clean round starts.
attributes.@qa09: FAIL → FIXED: Transition-only amber logic implemented. prevAmberPairsRef pattern is reusable in future ordering games.

ops.insert: SLS-M4 · Amber VO copy — all 14 lines audited for fairness / comparison language

attributes.@qa08: Full audit of all 14 amber lines. R0–R9, R11–R13 all compliant: direction-free, no totals, no >/< comparison, no who-goes-first language, no fairness implication. R10 VIOLATION: 'I think someone nearby has more snacks than me' — banned by permanent design rule (implies directional comparison, hints at who-has-more). Fixed to: 'Hmm, I'm not sure we've all been counted up properly.' — pure recomputation prompt with no directional hint. Zero fairness language detected in any UI copy, rule reminder, idle screen, or HUD text.
attributes.@qa09: FAIL → FIXED: R10 amber line replaced. 13/14 lines were compliant. Cross-cutting language audit passes for fairness and ordering-direction hints.

ops.insert: SLS-M5 · M1 trap coverage — 7/14 rounds (50%) target M1

attributes.@qa08: Spec requires ≥30% M1 trap rounds. M1 trap rounds: R3, R4, R6, R8, R10, R11, R13 = 7/14 = 50% ✓. All 7 traps verified: each has at least one pair where the bigger first addend corresponds to a smaller total. R10 is a 'double trap' (both 4+0 and 3+0 trap). No false positives — all 7 clean rounds (R0,R1,R2,R5,R7,R9,R12) correctly have no M1 trap.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — 50% trap rate exceeds minimum. Double trap in R10 is the strongest in the set.

ops.insert: SLS-M6 · No-ties constraint — all rounds have strictly distinct totals

attributes.@qa08: Spec: all P1 rounds must have strictly distinct totals per round. No tie episodes (reserved for P2A). Verified: R0(3,2) R1(4,3) R2(2,3,5) R3(3,4,5) R4(3,4,5) R5(1,2,4) R6(2,4,5) R7(2,3,4) R8(1,3,5) R9(2,3,4,5) R10(1,3,4,5) R11(1,2,4,5) R12(1,2,3,4) R13(1,3,4,5). All distinct ✓. No ties in any round.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — no ties in any round. Tie mechanic correctly reserved for P2A.

ops.insert: SLS-M7 · Within-5 constraint — all expressions have total ≤5, addition only

attributes.@qa08: Spec: addition within 5 only. No subtraction. No totals above 5. All 14 rounds audited: maximum total in any expression is 5 (3+2, 4+1, 2+3, 1+3, 0+4). No expression uses subtraction. Format is consistently A+B. Addition-only constraint upheld P1-wide.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — all expressions conform to addition-within-5 constraint.

ops.insert: SLS-M8 · Ordering rule display — no ranking/direction giveaway in UI text

attributes.@qa08: Rule reminder: 'Line up from most snacks → least snacks (front → back)'. This is the ordering direction — required by spec (Gate 12: ordering-direction indicator must communicate the rule). No individual ranking numbers shown. No slot labels revealing position rank. Idle screen: 'the one with the most snacks goes first' — rule statement, not a ranking giveaway. WARN: the rule reminder is text-only. Spec (Section 3, Gate 12) requires an icon + VO cue readable by non-readers. The current text-only implementation is a P1 gap — the spec says 'cannot rely on text alone'. However: this is a code demo, not a final playtest build — icon + audio are P2 polish items.
attributes.@qa09: WARN (P2 item): ordering-direction indicator is text-only. Spec Gate 12 requires icon + VO readable by non-readers. Deferred to P2 art/audio pass.
